title: Transition Discovery Map

levels.name: Unobserved
levels.description: Element identified as possible but not yet represented by fixture data or receipts.

levels.name: Observed
levels.description: Element appears in discussion, fixture inventory, or early evidence.

levels.name: Partitioned
levels.description: Element has a distinct role or transition class boundary.

levels.name: Tested
levels.description: Element has executable tests or receipt-backed proof cases.

levels.name: Integrated
levels.description: Element participates in cross-stage/public proof surfaces.

levels.name: Unified
levels.description: Element participates in Stage 6 unified admissibility validation.

authority_boundary: formalism-tests produces receipts. Site publishes receipts. Site must not become the authority for receipts.

elements.name: Admissible Existence
elements.summary: Top-level admissibility envelope for unified gate containment and recoverability.

elements.name: Boundary Coherence
elements.summary: Coherence and purpose-convergence boundary tests integrated into reset/evolve behavior.

elements.name: Consequence Horizon Formalism
elements.summary: Consequence-horizon framing for irreversible transition pressure.

elements.name: Data Continuity
elements.summary: Continuation admissibility and data-role consequence binding.

elements.name: Inference Window
elements.summary: Stage 6 requires inference-window containment inside A_total.

elements.name: Reverse Entropy Bound
elements.summary: Stage 6 requires reverse entropy score to remain below RE_max.

elements.name: Governed Boundary Reset
elements.summary: Recoverable non-convergence routes to a governed reset instead of hard allow.

elements.name: Governed Boundary Evolution
elements.summary: Purpose/coherence failure with recoverability routes to governed evolution.

elements.name: AI Block Scope
elements.summary: AI block admissibility and scope-escape failure are represented in Stage 6.

elements.name: FinCo Chain
elements.summary: Consent/access/use/compensation chain integrity participates in Stage 6.

elements.name: Role Non-Transfer
elements.summary: Same data can produce different continuation decisions across roles.

elements.name: Local-Composite Non-Equivalence
elements.summary: Local allow plus local allow does not imply composite allow.

elements.name: Replay Non-Reversal
elements.summary: Replay reconstructs receipt state but does not reverse consequence.

elements.name: Representation Non-Consequence
elements.summary: Partially covered; direct representation-only receipts still needed.
